headline: CONFIRMATION. Trial Day 2 (April 28) opening arguments delivered: Musk counsel Steven Molo characterized defendants as having 'stolen a charity'; OpenAI counsel William Savitt countered 'Mr. Musk didn't get his way.' Two surviving claims (unjust enrichment, breach of charitable trust) seek $134B and removal of Altman/Brockman. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers makes final remedy ruling; jury advisory only. Liability phase to May 21. Procedural milestone — substantive verdict pending. Composite 27.5 (Developing band) appropriately reflects the unresolved governance controversy without prejudging outcome. Illinois SB3444 support (AI liability shield) registered as INT/ACC negative signal but already absorbed in current Developing-band positioning.

evidence.finding: Musk testimony April 28; case 'could change the path of AI'; jury verdict advisory only.
Opening statements: Molo 'stole a charity' Picasso analogy; Savitt 'Mr. Musk didn't get his way' counter-narrative.
Two claims survive: unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust. Seek $134B return + Altman/Brockman removal + reversion to nonprofit structure. Liability phase concludes May 21.
OpenAI backed Illinois SB3444 (AI liability shield for mass-casualty events); Anthropic opposed. Divergent governance positioning.

post_trial_re_queue: Mandatory re-queue after Judge Gonzalez Rogers issues remedy decision (expected liability phase to May 21 + remedy phase thereafter). Trial outcome is a deterministic INT/SYS inflection point.
